g.data-narration: Welcome to NoSQL Graph Databases. This module explores how graph databases like Neo4j handle connected data efficiently.
g.text: From relational limitations to graph-powered solutions

g.data-narration: NoSQL databases include four main types: document, key-value, column-family, and graph databases, each optimized for specific needs.
NoSQL databases solve RDBMS limitations with flexible schemas, horizontal scalability, and high performance for specific use cases.
Graph databases represent data as nodes (entities) and edges (relationships), enabling efficient traversal of connected networks.
In Neo4j's property graph model, both nodes and relationships can have properties (key-value pairs), labels, and types.
Neo4j is a native graph database with ACID compliance, native graph processing, and the Cypher query language.
g.text: Index-Free Adjacency: O(1) relationship traversal vs O(n) JOINs

g.data-narration: Index-free adjacency enables O(1) constant-time relationship traversal in Neo4j, while relational databases need O(n) JOIN operations.
Cypher uses ASCII-art syntax: parentheses () for nodes, arrows --> for relationships, and brackets [] for relationship details.
Core Cypher clauses include MATCH for patterns, WHERE for filtering, RETURN for results, and CREATE for new data.
The CREATE clause adds new nodes and relationships to the graph. Here's how to create a movie database with actors.
Graph data modeling requires thinking in connections: identify entities as nodes, define how they relate, and add properties.
g.text: Think connections, not tables! Design queries first, then model accordingly.

g.data-narration: Graph databases excel at social networks. Here's how to model people and their connections like KNOWS and FOLLOWS.
Neo4j includes built-in algorithms for shortest path finding, centrality measures, and network analysis.
Key takeaways: Graph databases excel at connected data, Neo4j provides ACID compliance with O(1) traversal using Cypher.

g.text: NoSQL databases offer flexible schemas and horizontal scalability
Graph databases store relationships directly for O(1) traversal
Neo4j uses Cypher with intuitive ASCII-art syntax

g.data-narration: For exams: Remember index-free adjacency, Cypher syntax, ACID properties, and when to choose graph over relational databases.
